reasoning:
The March 30, 2025 reference date coincides with the Q1 2025 quarter-end spike. Data retrieved from FRED shows the RRP value climbed from ~100-150 earlier in March to 291.8 on March 27 and 286.6 on March 28. Since March 30 is a Sunday, the comparison value is likely the Friday, March 28 value of ~286.6. This is a very elevated baseline due purely to quarter-end window dressing by financial institutions.

1) By 2025-04-06 (Sunday, resolving to April 4), the quarter-end effect will have fully unwound, dropping the RRP balance back to its baseline of ~100-150. The chance of it exceeding 286.6 is extremely low (0.05).
2) For 2025-04-29, the month-end may provide a minor bump, but month-end spikes are much smaller than quarter-end spikes, and with QT running, the baseline continues to drift lower. (0.10).
3) For 2025-06-28 (Saturday, maps to June 27), this is a quarter-end preview. Given the ongoing structural drain on RRP from quantitative tightening (QT), the baseline heading into late June will likely be even lower than in March. Even a comparable spike magnitude would likely fail to breach the 286.6 mark set when the baseline was higher. (0.22).
4) For 2025-09-26 and 2026-03-30, we must factor in rising deep uncertainty about Fed policy. The Fed may halt QT before RRP hits zero, or an economic shock could lead to renewed QE, fundamentally altering the RRP trajectory. As per standard forecasting principles, probabilities for these distant dates move progressively closer to 0.5 (0.35 and 0.45 respectively).
